hypothesis: 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets on Polymarket enable settlement manipulation, causing temporary price distortions in BTC spot during settlement events; algorithmic traders can exploit these predictable anomalies.
signal_logic: Trigger: 5 minutes before each Polymarket Bitcoin prediction market settlement (every hour on the hour). Direction: Short BTC spot (or perpetual) 5 minutes before settlement, close 2 minutes after settlement (6-minute holding window). Entry: when BTC price deviation from 1-hour moving average exceeds 1.5 standard deviations. Exit: at settlement time + 2 minutes. Position: 0.5x leverage to limit risk.
data_needed: Polymarket Bitcoin prediction market settlement timestamps (Polymarket API or scrape)
BTC/USDT 1-minute OHLCV from Binance or Bybit
BTC funding rate from Binance/Bybit (optional for carry)
backtest_approach: Backtest on BTC/USDT from Jan 2024 to Jul 2026 (Polymarket active period). Asset: only BTC. Frequency: trade hourly. Stop-loss: 0.5% of entry price. Evaluate: Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, win rate, average return per trade.

supporting_sources.title: Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
supporting_sources.why: Stanford study confirms that 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets are susceptible to settlement manipulation, supporting the hypothesis of temporary price distortions.

risk_factors: Polymarket liquidity changes or market closure
Overfitting to specific manipulation patterns that may disappear
confidence_reason: Academic paper provides theoretical backing, but actual profitability depends on real-time manipulation persistence and transaction costs; sample size limited to one market.
quick_start: 1. Register for Polymarket API (https://docs.polymarket.com/api) to fetch settlement events. 2. Download BTC 1-min OHLCV from Binance via ccxt or REST API. 3. Implement a Python script that checks 5 min before each hourly settlement, computes price deviation, and sends alert/trade signal.
